steps.vulnerability_name: Fix getUserNameFromJwtToken — verify signature before extracting subject
steps.description: Replace the unverified JWTParser.parse() call with JJWT's verifying parser so the subject is only returned from a cryptographically valid token.

steps.vulnerability_name: Remove algorithm-confusion helpers and rewrite validateJwtToken to enforce RS256 only
steps.description: Delete getKeyFromJkuHeader (arbitrary-URL SSRF) and getJwtSecret (HS256 algorithm-confusion via RSA public key). Replace the vulnerable validateJwtToken with a strict RS256 check using JJWT's verifyWith, which rejects HS256, alg:none, and external JKU headers.

steps.vulnerability_name: Remove unused Nimbus JWT/crypto imports from JwtProvider
steps.description: After removing the algorithm-confusion helpers, the MACVerifier, RSASSAVerifier, JWTParser, PlainJWT, SignedJWT, URI, URLConnection, and StandardCharsets imports are no longer referenced.
The dashboard endpoint was incorrectly marked .permitAll(), allowing unauthenticated access to user data. Change it to .authenticated() so the JWT filter is enforced.

steps.vulnerability_name: Add integration test verifying dashboard requires authentication
steps.description: New test that confirms an unauthenticated GET /identity/api/v2/user/dashboard returns HTTP 401.

steps.vulnerability_name: Fix getUserFromToken — replace unreachable ParseException catch with RuntimeException
steps.description: getUserNameFromJwtToken no longer throws checked ParseException; it now throws JwtException (unchecked). Replace the catch so invalid tokens still result in EntityNotFoundException instead of a compile error.

steps.vulnerability_name: Fix getUserFromTokenWithoutValidation — replace unreachable ParseException catch with RuntimeException
steps.description: Same as step 6 but for the getUserFromTokenWithoutValidation method, which also calls getUserNameFromJwtToken and previously caught its now-removed checked exception.

steps.vulnerability_name: Remove unused ParseException import from UserServiceImpl
steps.description: After replacing both catch blocks, java.text.ParseException is no longer referenced in UserServiceImpl.

steps.vulnerability_name: Remove stale throws ParseException from JwtAuthTokenFilter.getUserFromToken
steps.description: getUserNameFromJwtToken no longer throws ParseException, so getUserFromToken's throws declaration is stale. Removing it stops the caller (UserServiceImpl) from being required to catch a checked exception that can never be thrown.
steps.search_code: public String getUserFromToken(HttpServletRequest request) throws ParseException {

steps.vulnerability_name: Remove unused ParseException import from JwtAuthTokenFilter
steps.description: After removing the throws declaration, java.text.ParseException is no longer referenced in JwtAuthTokenFilter.

verification.description: HS256 algorithm-confusion token must be rejected — forge an HS256 token signed with the RSA public key and confirm 401
alg:none (unsigned) token must be rejected — send a PlainJWT and confirm 401
JKU header injection must be rejected — token with a jku header pointing to attacker-controlled URL must return 401
Unauthenticated request to dashboard must return 401 (no token at all)
Valid RS256 token must still be accepted — authenticated request returns 200
